results.title: Actions against Senior Management and Technical Personnel for Lapses Reported by Transport Safety Investigation Bureau for September 2024 Train Incident
results.summary: For the September 2024 East-West Line disruption, the Land Transport Authority imposed a $2.4 million financial penalty on SMRT for breaching its Rapid Transit Systems Act licence. Additionally, SMRT was directed to invest at least $600,000 to strengthen capabilities and address identified improvements. The Transport Safety Investigation Bureau and LTA found no criminal wrongdoing by any individual, meaning no further government action against specific personnel is warranted. While LTA does not interfere with rail operators' internal human resource decisions, the operator may take its own actions based on investigation findings.

results.title: Additional Support for Stay-at-home Mothers in Retirement Adequacy and Career Development
results.summary: Stay-at-home mothers can access employment facilitation and reskilling initiatives, including Workforce Singapore's HerCareer programme and Career Conversion Programmes offering flexi-load roles. Those with lower retirement savings may benefit from enhanced measures like the Matched Retirement Savings Scheme and Silver Support Scheme, updated in January 2025. The Government will continue reviewing support measures for caregivers to ensure assistance across different life stages. Specific details on training access pathways and exact eligibility conditions for these schemes were not elaborated in the response.

results.title: Addressing Challenges that Prevent Special Needs Young Adults from Securing and Retaining Employment
results.summary: Challenges preventing special needs young adults from securing employment include employer unawareness of inclusive hiring benefits, limited workplace accessibility, and insufficient job coaching. To address these, the Government introduced the Employer Development Grant from 2025 to build employer capabilities and scale up hiring. The Open Door Programme offers customised job coach support and grants for job redesign, while the Enabling Employment Credit provides wage offsets. SG Enable's Enabling Business Hub delivers customised logistics training and intensive job support. A sectoral approach targets six key industries: food and beverage, retail, information and communications, health and social services, transport and storage, and financial and insurance services. Short-term assignments and gig jobs serve as stepping stones or flexible alternatives. The Job Coach Career Map launched in September 2024 supports professional development, with future learning journeys planned for job coaches across sectors to foster an inclusive workplace culture.

results.title: 100% Tariff on Pharmaceutical Imports to the US and its Impact on Singapore Economy
results.summary: On 25 September 2025, the US announced a 100% tariff on branded or patented pharmaceutical products effective 1 October 2025, unless manufacturers build US plants, though implementation was delayed for exemption negotiations. Singapore’s pharmaceutical exports to the US averaged S$3.7 billion annually between 2022 and 2024, with active pharmaceutical ingredients comprising 19% of total pharmaceutical exports to the US in 2024. The Government is negotiating preferential tariff arrangements with the US administration while continuing to attract investments under the Research, Innovation and Enterprise 2025 Plan, which includes a S$28 billion commitment to research and innovation. Long-term impacts on investment and drug pricing remain uncertain as negotiations continue, with the Economic Strategy Review developing recommendations to grow the economy amid global uncertainty.

results.title: Adjusting Procurement of Key Platforms to Ensure SAF's Readiness and Agility
results.summary: The Ministry of Defence monitors emerging security threats and technologies, noting the SAF has operated drones since the late 1980s. Unmanned systems are being integrated with relevant training provided to Full-Time and Operationally Ready National Servicemen, with more units and personnel expected to be equipped in coming years. MINDEF and the SAF will continue to invest the defence budget prudently and consistently to build capabilities systematically and sustainably, balancing fiscal sustainability with operational needs and international commitments.

results.title: Admission Requirements and Faculty Profile for Singapore College of Islamic Studies
results.summary: The Singapore College of Islamic Studies (SCIS) is being established under a Steering Committee chaired by Mufti Dr Nazirudin Mohd Nasir to determine admission criteria, curriculum, and faculty appointments. Financial support mechanisms, including scholarships, are currently being defined alongside course fees. The institution will partner with local and international entities, such as the Singapore University of Social Sciences for social sciences and Dar al-Ifta in Egypt for Islamic studies. Specific admission requirements, faculty profiles, and scholarship details will be announced prior to the first student intake in 2028.

results.title: Advertisement Campaign Describing Obesity as Chronic Disease
results.summary: The Health Sciences Authority (HSA) confirmed that a pharmaceutical campaign describing obesity as a chronic disease did not contravene regulations, as educational materials promoting public health purposes are permissible without naming specific medicines. HSA received no feedback indicating non-compliance and found no regulatory breaches in the advertisements displayed at One-North MRT station and other public locations. The authority advised consumers to consult healthcare professionals for managing obesity rather than relying on specific promotional claims. No further investigation or findings were shared as no violations were identified.

results.title: Aligning Penalties for Possessing Manufacturing Equipment under Misuse of Drugs Act 1973 with Guns, Explosive and Weapons Control Act 2021
results.summary: The Ministry for Home Affairs stated that the Misuse of Drugs Act 1973 does not criminalise the manufacturing of drug delivery systems, as items like syringes have legitimate medical uses. Consequently, the government assesses current provisions as adequate and has determined that aligning penalties with the Guns, Explosive and Weapons Control Act 2021 is unnecessary. No new measures or timelines for legislative changes were announced.

results.title: Alternative Entry Routes to Secondary Schools alongside PSLE
results.summary: The Minister for Education stated that the Ministry of Education (MOE) has introduced changes to the Primary School Leaving Examination (PSLE) scoring system to reduce fine differentiation and over-emphasize academic grades. An alternative pathway, Direct School Admission, allows students to enter secondary schools based on interests and aptitude beyond PSLE performance. MOE is currently studying alternative school posting approaches, including a primary-to-secondary through-train model and weighted school-based assessments, to evaluate their benefits and challenges. Key considerations include ensuring fair and transparent student posting and using these approaches as checkpoints to gauge learning and right-site students to appropriate subject levels at the start of secondary education. The Ministry will continue to study options to achieve these objectives better.

results.title: Analysing Mental Health Issues Faced by Students
results.summary: The Ministry of Education (MOE) monitors student mental health using internal data and recent studies, including the 2020 Youth Epidemiology and Resilience Study and the 2024 National Youth Mental Health Study. These insights inform measures such as refreshed Mental Health Education lessons, the Parenting for Wellness initiative, and collaboration with the Ministry of Health and Ministry of Social and Family Development under the National Mental Health and Well-being Strategy. While MOE does not hold specific national suicide rate data for youths aged 10 to 29, it employs a tiered intervention ecosystem. This includes equipping teachers with counselling skills, deploying school counsellors for risk assessments, and coordinating with REACH teams and hospital specialists for early intervention and support.

results.title: Applications for Licence to Operate Heavy Vehicle Parking Place
results.summary: Over the period 2022 to 2024, the Land Transport Authority received an average of approximately 700 annual new applications for licences to operate private Heavy Vehicle Parks, distinct from parking lot licences. Currently, about 2,100 operators hold these licences and issue Vehicle Parking Certificates. The mean and median processing time for these applications has remained consistent at roughly two days across the three-year period.

results.title: Appropriate Punishment and Rehabilitative Programmes for Young Offenders Convicted for Crimes of Mischief
results.summary: The Courts balance punishment and rehabilitation for young offenders convicted of mischief by considering offence severity, maturity, and reoffending risk. Serious offenders may receive probation, youth home placement, Reformative Training, or imprisonment, all involving structured programmes that combine discipline with education and therapy. Rehabilitation interventions specifically target emotional regulation, decision-making, prosocial skills, and family relationships, supported by probation officers, social workers, and community partners. From 2019 to 2023, the youth offending rate averaged 5.2 per 1,000 youths, with two-year recidivism rates remaining below 10% for cohorts up to 2021. The Ministry of Social and Family Development will continue collaborating with agencies and community partners to sustain these low rates.

results.title: Approval Process for Requests for Air-conditioners to be Installed in HDB Rental Flats
results.summary: As of August 2025, approximately 7,000 public rental flats have approved air-conditioner installations. The Housing and Development Board assesses applications based on tenant circumstances, prioritizing medical grounds, and issues approvals within two weeks if technical guidelines are met. Requests are evaluated individually rather than through a standardized quota system.

results.title: Assurances that Singtel's Singapore Operations are not Exposed to Systemic Risks from Procedural Lapses following Network Failure at Australian Subsidiary Optus
results.summary: The Minister clarified that Singapore's 999/995 emergency systems differ from Australia's 000 service, eliminating exposure to the same systemic risks as the Optus failure. Telecommunications providers must adhere to resilience requirements, including minimum coverage, Quality of Service standards, and regular audits. In the event of a disruption, providers are required to expeditiously restore services and implement rectification measures to prevent recurrence. All incidents will be investigated, with firm enforcement action taken where necessary to ensure critical infrastructure remains resilient.

results.title: Average Classroom Instruction Hours versus Total Working Hours for Teachers and Initiatives to Reduce Administrative Workload in Past Five Years
results.summary: Over the past five years, teachers' reported term-time work hours have remained stable at an average of 53 hours weekly, comprising approximately 14 hours of classroom instruction and 4 hours of administrative tasks. This represents a reduction from 5 hours of administrative work in 2021, achieved through streamlined processes, digital platforms like Parents Gateway, and dedicated administrative staff teams handling logistics and procurement. The Ministry of Education continues to leverage technology, including artificial intelligence solutions, to further streamline workflows and reduce administrative burdens. Specific quantitative targets for these initiatives were not provided in the response.

results.title: Average Population-to-facility Size Ratio for Polyclinics and Ratio for Polyclinics in Sengkang and Punggol
results.summary: The Ministry of Health (MOH) does not utilize an average population-to-facility size ratio for polyclinic development. Instead, MOH determines development plans by projecting service demand based on resident age profiles, private clinic availability, and proximity to other facilities. Consequently, specific ratios for polyclinics in Sengkang and Punggol are not calculated or disclosed. The stated approach prioritizes these contextual factors over a standardized population metric for planning purposes.

results.title: Basic Healthcare Sum Adequacy for Singaporeans at Age 65 and Plans to Review Quantum
results.summary: In 2024, 55% of active Singaporean CPF members aged 65 accumulated MediSave monies up to the Basic Healthcare Sum (BHS). The BHS is reviewed annually to ensure adequacy for basic healthcare needs from age 65. The Coordinating Minister for Social Policies and Minister for Health confirmed this annual review process but did not disclose specific plans for future adjustments or the exact number of members currently below the threshold.

results.title: Breakdown of BTO Flat Launches by Flat Type in 2025 to 2027 and over Last Five Years
results.summary: The Minister for National Development provided the breakdown of Build-To-Order (BTO) flat launches by flat type from 2020 to 2025. The 2025 figures include indicative supply for the October 2025 exercise, with exact details to be shared during the sales launch. Specific supply data for 2026 and 2027 will be released when ready. The government will continue to monitor the housing market and calibrate BTO supply to meet Singaporeans' needs.

results.title: Breakdown of BTO and Sales of Balance Flats by Applicant Category over Past 10 Years
results.summary: Tables 1 and 2 present the number of successful Build-To-Order (BTO) and Sale of Balance Flats (SBF) applicants from 2015 to 2024. The data is categorized by applicant type, including seniors, first-timer families, first-timer singles, and second-timer families, and further broken down by flat size: 2-room flexi, 3-room, and larger flats. This breakdown covers the requested ten-year period for both BTO and SBF launches.

results.title: Cases of Jaywalking and Fatal Accidents Related to Jaywalking
results.summary: In the first half of 2025, Traffic Police detected approximately 730 jaywalking cases, a 4% rise from the prior year, alongside nine fatal pedestrian accidents compared to seven previously. Between January 2024 and September 2025, authorities conducted 25 elderly-focused and roughly 640 general pedestrian road safety events reaching over 100,000 individuals. These outreach initiatives emphasize using designated crossings, maintaining alertness to traffic, and avoiding distractions like mobile devices while crossing. The Minister did not confirm plans to impose stiffer penalties or detail further educational campaign strategies beyond the reported events.

results.title: Cases where Students Physically Assaulted Teachers or School Staff
results.summary: Cases of students physically assaulting teachers or school staff remain rare, though incidents involving staff intervening in fights or managing students with Special Educational Needs are included. Schools address such behaviour through established disciplinary processes, including suspension and caning for boys, alongside filing police reports where necessary. Preventive measures involve Character and Citizenship Education to foster pro-social behaviour and relationship management skills. Corrective actions include counselling and collaborative efforts with parents to rebuild respectful relationships. Staff are trained to de-escalate situations while prioritising the safety and well-being of all students and staff.

results.title: Clarification by Senior Minister of State for National Development
results.summary: Senior Minister of State for National Development Ms Sun Xueling clarified that HDB has acquired seven previously sold shops in Yishun, Pasir Ris, Jurong West, and Choa Chu Kang. These acquisitions were conducted for time-sensitive upgrading and reconfiguration works. This information addresses a previous query regarding selective acquisitions of HDB sold shops. No further details on future acquisition plans or specific timelines were provided in this statement.

results.title: Clarifying Tax Treatment of Quasi-structural Assets
results.summary: The Ministry of Finance will update the Inland Revenue Authority of Singapore's e-Tax Guide to reflect clarifications on capital allowance eligibility for quasi-structural assets like cement silos and airport runways. These rulings explicitly define assets that do not qualify for capital allowances, addressing recent adverse rulings. The Ministry stated it regularly reviews tax policies considering evolving business needs and jurisprudence to maintain relevance. No specific timeline for the guide update was provided in the transcript.

results.title: Clinical Pathways Linking Schools and Primary Care to Emergency Departments for Adolescents at Risk of Self-harm
results.summary: The Ministry of Health (MOH) and Ministry of Education (MOE) currently lack a unified national crisis pathway or specific Key Performance Indicators (KPIs) for suicide risk intervention, as setting such metrics is deemed impractical due to the individualized nature of building trust and rapport. Instead, the Practice Guide for the Tiered Care Model directs students at imminent risk to hospital emergency departments, while those with non-imminent risk are referred to Response, Early intervention and Assessment in Community (REACH) teams. The national mindline 1771 serves as the primary first-stop for anonymous, non-judgmental counselling and referrals for all age groups, with access via phone, text, or WhatsApp. As of March 2025, approximately 140,000 frontliners have received basic mental health training, and MOH continues to collaborate with agencies to ensure timely support without imposing artificial constraints on care delivery.

results.title: Collaborating with Agro-food Enterprises Federation to Aggregate, Brand and Market Local Produce
results.summary: The Singapore Food Agency (SFA) collaborates with the Singapore Agro-Food Enterprises Federation (SAFEF) to aggregate local produce, facilitating long-term commercial contracts between farmers and retailers. This partnership has established branded lines such as "SG Farmers' Market" for leafy vegetables and "The Straits Fish" for seafood, available at major supermarkets like FairPrice and Giant. Since May 2024, sales of xiao bai cai under the "SG Farmers' Market" brand have increased approximately seven-fold, while "The Straits Fish" dishes were launched in 2024 across Ming Kitchen Seafood and the "zichar" chain. In 2025, SAFEF partnered with Coriander Leaf restaurant to integrate locally farmed marine tilapia into their menu. Additionally, SAFEF operates the SG Farmers' Market weekend pop-up events to promote local farms in heartlands. SFA and SAFEF continue to expand the range of aggregated produce and explore initiatives to drive demand, which is essential for farms to achieve scale and safeguard Singapore's food security.

results.title: Comparison of Infants Enrolled in ECDA Childminding Pilot Scheme versus Centre-based Infant Care Services
results.summary: Over 80% of infants enrolled in the ECDA Childminding Pilot Scheme utilise full-day care, compared to nearly 100% in centre-based infant care services. Additionally, more than 80% of enrolled families have used their Child Development Account monies to pay for these childminding services. The Minister noted that while the pilot scheme predominantly features full-day arrangements, the usage of savings accounts for funding remains high across both models.

results.title: Converting Dead Spaces Below Flyovers, Viaducts, MRT Tracks or Bridges into Space for Sporting Facilities
results.summary: Sport Singapore plans to convert underutilised dead spaces, such as those under flyovers, viaducts, and MRT tracks, into sheltered multi-purpose sporting facilities to address rising demand and climate-related weather disruptions. Existing examples include the Jalan Bukit Merah CTE viaduct touch tennis court and the upcoming Gali Batu Flyover courts for futsal and pickleball. The agency is also repurposing multi-storey car park rooftops, like the upcoming Dover Crescent facility, and will continue collaborating with other agencies to identify further opportunities for utilising these spaces.

results.title: Criteria for Allocating and Prioritising Admission to ECDA's Inclusive Support Programme
results.summary: The Early Childhood Development Agency's Inclusive Support Programme (InSP) allocates places based on paediatrician-assessed developmental needs, preschool capacity, class matching, and parental location preference. Eligibility is restricted to Singapore Citizens and Permanent Residents; priority for citizens is not explicitly stated. Approximately 600 new InSP places will be progressively added from 2026 onwards across 15 to 20 preschools, supplementing the existing six pilot sites. The Ministry will guide the geographic spread of these new places using projected demand for early intervention services. As the programme scales, demand assessment will continue to ensure a healthy supply of available places.

results.title: Data for Cases Directed to Community Mediation Centre and Community Disputes Resolution Tribunals
results.summary: The Minister for Law outlined the Government's approach to managing community disputes following the passage of the Community Disputes Resolution (Amendment) Act 2024, which enhanced the Community Disputes Management Framework. The strategy prioritizes fostering positive neighbourly relations through informal mediation by community leaders or HDB officers at an early stage before disputes escalate. When informal efforts fail, residents are encouraged to participate in free formal mediation at the Community Mediation Centre (CMC), a process proven effective for resolving disputes while preserving long-term neighbourly relations. The Minister noted that compulsory attendances at the CMC are directed through various channels, including authorised officers, tribunals, and police referrals, though specific statistics for the last three years were not detailed in this excerpt.

results.title: Data for Wage Offsets Received through Uplifting Employment Credit Scheme
results.summary: Since launching in April 2023, the Uplifting Employment Credit (UEC) scheme has provided wage offsets of up to 20% of monthly income for nine months to employers hiring ex-offenders. As of December 2024, approximately 1,200 employers received offsets for hiring over 3,200 ex-offenders, primarily in administrative, support, and accommodation/food services sectors. Roles typically include drivers, cleaning supervisors, and hotel cleaners. Of the more than 1,500 hires between April and December 2023, over half remained employed after the nine-month support period ended. The Ministry for Manpower will continue monitoring these outcomes to support ex-offender reintegration.

results.title: Data on Accidents where Foreign Work Pass Holders are Involved
results.summary: From 2021 to 2024, foreign Work Pass holders were involved in an average of 1,489 road accidents annually, representing 22% of all accidents and 13% of fatal accidents. During this period, an average of 15 fatalities occurred in accidents involving these drivers. The Coordinating Minister for National Security and Minister for Home Affairs provided these statistics, which cover the years 2021 through 2024. No further breakdown by specific year or additional operational constraints were stated in the response.

results.title: Data on Applications for MAS' FinTech Regulatory Sandbox Framework
results.summary: The Monetary Authority of Singapore (MAS) reports a median processing time of three months for its FinTech Regulatory Sandbox applications. Of the 19 firms accepted, 15 graduated to obtain relevant licences, while one ceased operations by September. No approvals were granted for Sandbox Plus due to existing regulatory frameworks covering most proposed technologies. Some applicants were not accepted because their business models already complied with current regulations.

results.title: Data on Cases Related to Offence of Deliberately Wounding Racial Feelings in Last Two Years and Strengthening Inter-faith Harmony following Such Incidents
results.summary: In response to inquiries regarding the offence of deliberately wounding racial feelings, the Coordinating Minister for National Security and Minister for Home Affairs confirmed that the Police investigated approximately 25 race- and religion-related offences annually over the last two years. The Minister highlighted a robust legal framework comprising the Penal Code, Maintenance of Religious Harmony Act, and Maintenance of Racial Harmony Act, noting that specific threats to places of worship trigger immediate police action, including investigations and enhanced security patrols. Upstream efforts under the SGSecure movement involve the Ministry of Culture, Community and Youth strengthening religious organisations' crisis response capabilities, while community initiatives like the Racial and Religious Harmony Circles foster intercultural understanding through dialogues and celebrations. Following the Al-Istiqamah Mosque incident, leaders from various faith communities issued statements condemning the act and expressing solidarity, underscoring the collective resilience of Singapore's society against threats to racial and religious harmony.

results.title: Data on Childhood Obesity Rates by Age Group, Ethnicity and Social Income, and Enhancing Measures to Prevent and Reduce Childhood Obesity
results.summary: In 2024, the proportion of children across primary, secondary, and pre-university levels with a BMI-for-age at or above the 90th percentile is 13%. The Government monitors obesity rates by age, ethnicity, and social income, though specific breakdowns were not provided in the response. To prevent and reduce childhood obesity, the Ministry implements Grow Well SG, which promotes nutrition, sleep, screen use, and physical activity through lifestyle prescriptions for students and resources for parents. This strategy complements the Health Promotion Board's Healthy Meals in Schools Programme, MOVE IT programmes, and the Ministry of Education's Physical Education lessons. Additionally, KidSTART supports families with fewer resources in early childhood development.

results.title: Criteria that Qualify for Intervention by Community Relations Unit and Unit's Effectiveness in Addressing Neighbourly Disputes
results.summary: The Community Relations Unit (CRU) will expand its pilot programme beyond Tampines to private estates, implementing a tiered intervention framework where straightforward cases receive abatement orders without formal mediation, while cases involving threats of violence trigger mandatory Mediation Directions. Public officers from HDB and CRU will assess dispute severity to determine appropriate responses, supported by predictive analytics for early identification and an early warning system for violent disputes. Legislative reviews are underway to establish specific noise decibel thresholds and hoarding criteria, alongside plans to digitise CDRT evidence submission and enhance grassroots mediator safety. Enforcement of Community Disputes Resolution Tribunal orders remains the responsibility of affected neighbours, though structural root causes like soundproofing subsidies and mandatory logging will be addressed to reduce future disputes.

results.title: Data on Drone Incursions on Singapore's Aerodromes over Past Six Years and Impact on National Security
results.summary: Over the past six years, two drone incursions occurred at Changi Airport in 2019, causing flight delays and diversions, while no incursions were recorded at Seletar Airport. The Acting Minister for Transport confirmed there was no evidence these incidents posed national security threats. The Civil Aviation Authority of Singapore (CAAS) enforces a five-kilometre no-fly zone around aerodromes and collaborates with agencies like the Singapore Police Force and Singapore Armed Forces to detect and neutralise unlawful drones. This multi-layered strategy combines regulations, education, and enforcement to ensure aviation safety and counter drone incursions effectively.

results.title: Civil Aviation Authority of Singapore (Amendment) Bill
results.summary: The Civil Aviation Authority of Singapore (Amendment) Bill establishes a pragmatic framework to enable Sustainable Aviation Fuel (SAF) adoption through a fixed cost envelope model and centralised procurement. While supporting the Bill's intent to decarbonise aviation beyond national carbon budgets, Members seek clarifications on the SAF levy's fund mechanism, transparency, and governance to ensure cost stability and prevent carbon leakage. Key concerns focus on whether the flat passenger levy adequately addresses SAF price volatility, impacts cargo competitiveness, and ensures equitable cost sharing across flight classes. The government must define triggers for future levy adjustments, specify feedstock standards, and outline public reporting obligations for the SAF Fund to maintain Singapore's status as a regional hub.

results.title: Data on Foreign-registered Vehicles by Type Entering Singapore and Plans for Traffic Management
results.summary: In 2024, approximately 77,000 foreign-registered vehicles entered Singapore daily, with motorcycles accounting for 82% of this total, representing about 8% of the local vehicle population. Entry is regulated via Vehicle Entry Permits (VEP) for cars and motorcycles, and ASEAN Goods Vehicle Permits (GVP) for goods vehicles, both tied to road tax and insurance with validity up to one year or six months. To reduce demand for private vehicles, the Johor-Singapore Rapid Transit Link will offer seamless travel through co-located Customs, Immigration, and Quarantine facilities at departure points. Additionally, the Woodlands RTS station will connect directly to the Thomson-East Coast Line MRT station to improve network access.

results.title: Data on HDB Flat Application Status Following Death of Sole Citizen Parent Over Past Five Years and Appeal Mechanisms Available
results.summary: The Minister stated that HDB adopts a compassionate approach to family structure changes following a parent's death, prioritizing the welfare of surviving minor Singapore Citizen children. If the death occurs after key collection, a surviving Singapore Permanent Resident parent is eligible to retain the flat with the child. In other cases, HDB assesses assistance individually, which may include holding the flat in trust for transfer to the child at age 21. The Minister clarified that specific data on cancellation counts broken down by timing is unavailable because HDB does not track "death of a sole SC parent" as a distinct cancellation reason.

results.title: Data on Home-based Food Companies Registered Yearly since COVID-19 and Enforcement of Safety Measures for Home-based Food Businesses
results.summary: The Singapore Food Agency (SFA) does not track the number of home-based food businesses because they are not required to be licensed. These businesses must comply with existing food safety regulations, and SFA will enforce action against any sale of unsafe food. Regarding fire safety, the Singapore Civil Defence Force imposes no additional requirements beyond standard residential standards due to the small-scale nature of these operations. Consequently, no specific yearly registration data or additional safety measures beyond current laws are in place for these entities.

results.title: Data on MediSave Adequacy at Age 65 across Income Levels and Proportion of Lower-income Seniors at Risk of Exhausting MediSave Balances
results.summary: The Minister clarified that studying MediSave adequacy at age 65 is not meaningful due to significant variations in healthcare needs occurring later in life. While lower-income seniors may save less, they benefit from higher subsidies and initiatives like the MediSave Bonus and the Matched MediSave Scheme starting in 2026. Safety nets such as MediFund ensure no one loses access to healthcare due to exhausted balances or financial difficulties. The Ministry noted that individual utilisation, lifespan, and health status heavily influence account sufficiency rather than age alone.

results.title: Data on Neighbour Dispute Cases in Tampines HDB Town and Effectiveness of Community Relations Unit since April 2025
results.summary: The Minister for National Development stated that the question regarding neighbour dispute cases in Tampines HDB town and the effectiveness of the Community Relations Unit (CRU) since its April 2025 pilot launch was already addressed in a reply provided on 14 October 2025. The response covered the number of reported cases, CRU operational criteria, and the timeline for nationwide rollout. No new data or updates were presented during this session.

results.title: Data on Private Heavy Vehicle Parking Lots Not Accessible to Public
results.summary: Approximately 27,000 private heavy vehicle parking lots, representing about 90% of such facilities, do not allow the public to enquire about parking space availability. The Acting Minister for Transport clarified that Heavy Vehicle Park licence holders retain full discretion to manage vehicle parking certificate allocation and may or may not directly own heavy vehicles. Consequently, the specific number of heavy vehicles registered to these non-disclosing licence holders was not provided in the response.

results.title: Designating Rooftop Gardens as No-smoking Areas
results.summary: The National Environment Agency currently prohibits smoking in rooftop gardens within or connected to residential buildings due to high resident footfall, aligning them with other common areas. However, smoking remains permitted on rooftop gardens above multi-storey car parks, as these locations do not meet the criteria of frequent family use or enclosed spaces. The Ministry has agreed to consider designating these car park rooftops as no-smoking areas while ongoing reviews of smoking prohibition regulations continue.

results.title: Differentiated Discount Rate for Travel during Peak and Off-peak Periods for PWD Concession Scheme
results.summary: The Acting Minister for Transport confirmed that persons with disabilities currently receive up to 55% off adult per-journey fares. Additionally, eligible commuters can save an extra 50 cents on Mass Rapid Transit fares when traveling before 7.45 am on weekdays. They may also purchase monthly concession passes for $58, providing unlimited travel on trains and basic bus services. The Minister did not state whether the Government will implement a differentiated discount rate for peak and off-peak periods as requested.

results.title: Digital Parenting Survey's Representation of Resident Parent Population's Income Levels
results.summary: The inaugural Digital Parenting Survey sample represents Singapore's resident parent population by gender, age, and race. However, the Ministry cannot assess representativeness regarding income levels because this specific data is not collected or made available through the survey. Consequently, the survey's findings do not reflect the income distribution of the resident parent population.

results.title: Discrepancy between Amount Budgeted for Voucher Schemes and Actual Amount Disbursed
results.summary: The Minister explained that assessing voucher scheme take-up is more meaningful through overall utilisation across validity periods rather than annual budget utilisation. Utilisation rates vary significantly, ranging from 23% for Climate Friendly Households Programme Vouchers in Phase 1 to over 90% for Community Development Council Vouchers issued up to 2024. The SingapoRediscovers Vouchers achieved 56% utilisation, while SG60 vouchers were only issued in the current year. Specific discrepancies between budgeted and disbursed amounts for fiscal years 2019 to 2024 were not provided.

results.title: Disincentives for Commerical Property Vacancies and Enhancing Transparency of Retail Market Data
results.summary: The Minister stated that commercial and residential vacancy rates have remained stable over the past three years, noting that landlords generally avoid prolonged vacancies due to holding costs and forgone rental income. He clarified that the Government assesses market conditions using a variety of factors beyond vacancy rates alone, as these metrics vary across property segments and time due to cyclical demand shifts. Regarding data transparency, the Urban Redevelopment Authority currently publishes detailed rental data quarterly by geographical location and property type, alongside information on vacant commercial properties. The Minister did not specify a particular vacancy percentage or duration threshold that would trigger Government intervention, nor did he confirm plans to release unit-level retail rental data or monthly granular trends by planning area or property size.

results.title: Effectiveness of "No Smoking Zone" in Orchard Road
results.summary: The National Environment Agency (NEA) has not conducted further surveys on the Orchard Road No Smoking Zone since July 2019. Discounting 2021 safe distancing measures, feedback on smoking cases remained stable between 2022 and 2024, averaging fewer than 30 cases per month. This volume represents less than 2% of total smoking feedback received by NEA in prohibited places. The agency will continue monitoring the zone's effectiveness. No specific percentage reduction in feedback since 2021 was stated.

results.title: Effectiveness of Safety Campaigns for Delivery Riders
results.summary: The Traffic Police will consider engaging platform companies to share data on delivery riders' habits to assess safety campaign effectiveness and identify high-risk behaviors. Existing public education efforts by the Singapore Road Safety Council and Land Transport Authority will continue alongside potential new interventions. For accidents involving injuries, the Traffic Police and Ministry of Manpower will investigate and enforce penalties under the Road Traffic Act and Workplace Safety and Health Act respectively. These laws apply to all road users, with no specific tracking of non-injury accidents currently in place.

results.title: Emergency Evacuation Protocols for High-rise Fires Involving Seniors or Residents with Mobility Impairments
results.summary: The Singapore Civil Defence Force (SCDF) prioritises evacuating occupants on the fire-affected floor and the two floors above, while advising residents on other floors to remain in their fire-compartmented units unless necessary. Seniors and residents with mobility impairments are instructed to stay in safe rooms, close doors, and await assistance rather than self-evacuating, aligning with international best practices to minimise injury risks. To support this, SCDF launched the "Fire Safety Kakis" volunteer initiative on 1 March 2025 with grassroots organisations to aid vulnerable residents. Super high-rise buildings over 40 storeys include refuge floors every 20 storeys as safe holding areas. For further details on community fire drills and evacuation chairs, members should refer to Parliamentary Questions filed for the Sittings on 23 and 24 September 2025.

results.title: Employers Issued Warnings or Made to Attend Mandatory Workshops due to Rejection of FWA Request without Adequate Justification
results.summary: The Minister for Manpower directed members to a written reply for specific statistics on employer warnings or mandatory workshops issued since December 2024 regarding unjustified Flexible Work Arrangement rejections. No new enforcement measures or timelines were announced in this session. Employers facing implementation challenges or employees with unconsidered requests are advised to approach the Tripartite Alliance for Fair and Progressive Employment Practices for assistance. The Minister did not confirm whether stronger enforcement measures will be considered beyond existing guidance.

results.title: Enforcement Efforts to Ensure Heavy Vehicles are Parked in Designated Spaces
results.summary: Under the Vehicle Parking Certificate (VPC) Scheme, heavy vehicles must park in authorised lots between 12.00 am and 6.00 am. The Land Transport Authority enforces this by issuing Notices of Offence for non-compliance based on patrols and feedback. From 2020 to 2024, the average annual number of such notices issued was approximately 1,300. The Minister confirmed these enforcement efforts target vehicles parked at non-designated locations outside the permitted early morning window.

results.title: Enhance Training for Healthcare Professionals Engaging Grieving Families of Deceased Organ Donors
results.summary: The Ministry of Health (MOH) provides regular training for healthcare professionals to communicate sensitively with families of deceased organ donors but has no plans to enhance these protocols. For citizens and permanent residents turning 21, MOH issues information packages on the Human Organ Transplant Act 1987 to enable informed decisions regarding compulsory donation provisions. Public awareness efforts, including the Live On Festival, aim to reach diverse segments, including older Singaporeans, ensuring they remain informed about their options to object or withdraw objections.

results.title: Enhancements to Extended Childcare Leave Scheme and Proposal of New Leave Scheme for Caregivers
results.summary: The Government will study enhancements to the Extended Childcare Leave scheme to increase reimbursed leave days, though no immediate change was confirmed. While a new Paid Caregiving Leave scheme for elderly parents was not introduced, the Minister noted that many employers voluntarily provide such leave. Recent measures include the Shared Parental Leave scheme, fully implemented from April next year, and the Tripartite Guidelines on Flexible Work Arrangements implemented in December 2024 to standardize FWA requests. The Government will balance employee caregiving needs with employer costs and manpower requirements during regular policy reviews, continuing to foster family-friendly workplaces through tripartite collaboration.

results.title: Enhancing Worker Transport Safety on Roads and at Work Sites
results.summary: The Workplace Safety and Health (WSH) Act mandates employers to ensure safe vehicle use, requiring proper maintenance and operator qualifications. The WSH Council provides guidelines on traffic management, forklift operation, and fatigue management, while the Productivity Solutions Grant funds technology like collision-warning cameras and fatigue detection sensors for local SMEs. For commuting risks, the Ministry of Transport employs a multi-pronged strategy to reduce lorry transport needs, promote alternatives, and enhance safety for necessary ferrying. The Ministry of Manpower and Transport collaborate to address both work-site and road incidents through these regulatory and technological measures.

results.title: Ensuring Child Development Account Funds Are Used to Meet Children's Legitimate Needs
results.summary: Effective 1 January 2026, Child Development Account (CDA) usage is discontinued at retail pharmacies and optical shops due to audit findings indicating a higher risk of misuse. This restriction does not affect pharmacy departments within healthcare institutions, hospitals, polyclinics, or Baby Bonus Approved Institutions, where CDA funds remain valid for medication, surgical products, and supplements. Concurrently, the Ministry expanded CDA eligibility in May to cover preschool incidental charges, including enrichment programmes like speech and drama. Parents may also utilise other Government support schemes, such as the Baby Bonus Cash Gift and Child LifeSG Credits, to defray child-raising costs, while lower-income families can seek assistance from Social Service Offices.

results.title: Evaluating Risky and Ineffective Airdrops of Humanitarian Aid to Intended Recipients
results.summary: The Ministry states that land transfer is the preferred humanitarian aid delivery model to Gaza, while airdrops serve as a last resort when land access is restricted. To mitigate risks, the Republic of Singapore Air Force collaborated with the Royal Jordanian Air Force to select safe drop zones and ensure civilian safety. Rigorous checks confirmed that parachutes and loads met established safety standards, with detailed planning ensuring aid lands within designated zones. The RSAF will continue these precautions if tasked with airdrops in the future.

results.title: Expanding CareShield Life Coverage to More Persons under Age 30 or Permit Exceptional Coverage on Appeal
results.summary: The Ministry will not expand CareShield Life coverage to persons under 30 or permit exceptional coverage on appeal. Mandatory enrolment begins at age 30 because premiums are paid during working years when MediSave savings are available, while severe disability typically occurs at older ages. Allowing earlier enrolment would cause adverse selection, where higher-risk individuals opt in, thereby increasing premiums for everyone. Young Singaporeans with disabilities requiring assistance can access other Government schemes such as the Home Caregiving Grant and MediFund instead.

results.title: Expanding Use of SG Culture Pass to Include Movie Screenings
results.summary: The Ministry of Culture, Community and Youth (MCCY) is exploring expanding the SG Culture Pass, launched on 1 September 2025, to include movie screenings. Discussions are underway with the Singapore International Film Festival regarding eligibility for local films written, directed, or produced by Singaporeans, scheduled for the end-November 2025 festival. Additionally, MCCY is assessing technical feasibility with cinema operators to onboard local film screenings onto the pass. The initiative aims to support local artists and works while enhancing access to local arts and heritage for Singaporeans.

results.title: Expansion of "Relevant Individuals" under Section 18(1)(a) of CPF Act 1953 to Include Uncles and Aunts
results.summary: The Minister clarified that while CPF voluntary transfers under Section 18(1)(a) are limited to immediate family members to safeguard retirement adequacy, uncles and aunts are already supported through existing flexibilities. Members can provide cash top-ups to these extended family members' CPF accounts. Additionally, if eligible for the Matched Retirement Savings Scheme, recipients receive dollar-for-dollar matching grants on these top-ups. This approach balances support for closest dependents with options to assist extended family without compromising retirement savings.

results.title: Expansion of Drug Rehabilitation Centre and Community Rehabilitation Centre Capacity if Etomidate-related Admissions Rise
results.summary: From 1 January 2020 to 31 December 2024, annual average admissions to Drug Rehabilitation Centres (DRC) and Community Rehabilitation Centres (CRC) were 2,000 and 35 respectively, with current utilisation at 76% and 28%. Etomidate was listed as a Class C controlled drug under the Misuse of Drugs Act 1973 from 1 September 2025, mandating DRC admission for third or subsequent offenders aged 16 and above. As of 1 October 2025, no etomidate-related DRC admissions have occurred. The Singapore Prison Service will monitor capacity closely and ensure sufficient resources for in-house rehabilitation programmes, including psychology-based and family support. Released etomidate abusers will undergo community supervision overseen by the Health Sciences Authority, with the Ministry of Health working with partners to sustain reintegration support.

results.title: Extending Central Kitchen Model Beyond Initial 13 Schools and Cost of Such Meals Compared to Cost under Traditional School Canteen Model
results.summary: The Ministry of Education will assess expanding the central kitchen model beyond the initial 13 schools starting January 2026 based on operational efficiency, cost-effectiveness, and student satisfaction. Expansion depends on schools' specific needs, readiness, and interest, while the existing Individual Stallholder System remains dominant in the short to medium term. Central kitchen operators must provide at least one full meal priced at no more than $2.70 for primary schools and $3.60 for secondary schools, rates comparable to individual stallholders. Students receiving MOE Financial Assistance Scheme (FAS) will have meals fully paid for, with additional support available for those ineligible. Affected stallholders will be facilitated to work in other schools or with incoming operators.

results.title: Financial Assistance for Next-of-kin Without Lasting Power of Attorney
results.summary: The Legal Aid Bureau (LAB) offers legal aid for deputyship applications to Singapore Citizens and Permanent Residents meeting means and merits tests. Eligible applicants receive legal representation for standard applications or self-file via the iFAMs system for simplified applications, with LAB assisting non-digital users. Those ineligible for government aid may seek advice at Community Law Centres through Pro Bono SG or various legal clinics. This support addresses financial hardship where next-of-kin lack a Lasting Power of Attorney to manage essential matters.

results.title: Government's Response to MRT Network's Reliability Falling to Lowest Level since 2020
results.summary: The Acting Minister for Transport stated that the Ministry's assessment of the MRT network's reliability for the 12 months ending June 2025, which fell to its lowest level since 2020, has been addressed in an oral response issued for Parliamentary Questions 19 to 24. This response was released for the 22 September 2025 Parliament Sitting. The Minister did not provide a separate assessment or satisfaction statement regarding the proactiveness of the maintenance regime for ageing lines within this specific exchange, as the matter was directed to the pre-issued oral response document.

results.title: Grassroots Leaders Securing Priority for their Children under Phase 2B of Primary 1 Registration Exercise
results.summary: Under the Primary 1 Registration Framework, current serving committee members of the Residents' Network, Residents' Committee, Neighbourhood Committee, Citizen's Consultative Committee, Community Club Management Committee, and Community Development Council are eligible to register their children under Phase 2B as active community leaders in schools within two kilometres of their residence. This eligibility extends beyond grassroots leaders to other volunteers connected to the school, provided they have served actively in these committees for at least two years continuously prior to the registration exercise. Applicants must submit their cases to the People's Association for assessment and endorsement, where success depends on annual demand and vacancies. The Acting Minister for Culture, Community and Youth stated that PA does not track the specific number of successful or unsuccessful applications, nor does it distinguish outcomes between ballot and non-ballot processes.

results.title: Guidelines for Approval of Renovation Works for Older HDB Flats to Ensure Structural Integrity and Prevent Damages
results.summary: The Minister for National Development stated that the Housing and Development Board applies identical structural safety standards and approval processes for renovation works across all HDB flats, regardless of age. Contractors must obtain HDB approval before commencing any works affecting the structure, with permits potentially including site-specific conditions such as limits on wall removal. Additionally, only contractors listed in the Directory of Renovation Contractors (DRC) are permitted to perform such works, and their workers must be trained by the Building and Construction Authority Academy for demolition or hacking. The Ministry confirmed it has no plans to introduce separate criteria for older flats, citing the current assessment approach as sufficiently robust.

results.title: Guidelines for Employers to Reduce "Ghost Job" Postings
results.summary: The Ministry of Manpower validates labour market statistics through stringent cross-checks against sources like MyCareersFuture, ensuring reported vacancies reflect genuine hiring activity. While multiple postings across portals are considered normal recruitment efforts rather than fictitious openings, the Ministry actively follows up with employers showing sharp vacancy rises without corresponding hires or repeated unfilled positions. To support jobseekers, the Ministry is enhancing MyCareersFuture with filters to identify responsive employers and will continue engaging employers to encourage responsible posting practices. The Minister clarified that current measures improve data quality and reliability, though specific new guidelines to reduce ghost jobs were not announced in this session.

results.title: Guidelines on Noise Allowance Standards and Installing Safety Enhancements and Sound Dampeners at Communal Sports Facilities
results.summary: The Minister for National Development stated that the question regarding noise guidelines for pickleball and converting HDB communal sports facilities to multi-purpose venues with sound dampeners was already addressed in a reply to Assoc Prof Jamus Jerome Lim on 25 September 2025. No new measures, timelines, or specific study convening were announced in this session. The response directs users to the previous parliamentary record for the relevant policy details and operational constraints.

results.title: Helping Youths Overcome Social Media and Gaming Addictions
results.summary: The Ministry of Health (MOH) does not currently track social media and gaming addictions among youths but is reviewing medical evidence and community support access with the National Council of Social Service. For youths unwilling to seek treatment, interventions require client cooperation; parents can access resources via Mindline.sg or contact the national helpline 1771 for guidance. Counsellors may facilitate initial befriending to build trust before engaging the youth in care, as forced intervention is generally not feasible. Available community programmes include those offered by TOUCH Community Services and WE CARE Community Services for those seeking voluntary help.

results.title: Funding Each Child: A Preschool Voucher Proposal
results.summary: The Government rejects replacing direct operator subsidies with a portable preschool voucher scheme, citing risks of fee escalation and reduced provider diversity. While acknowledging concerns over market distortion favoring Government-supported operators, the Ministry maintains that the current mix of supply-side grants, fee caps, and demand-side subsidies already ensures autonomy and competition. Officials assert that voucher supplements alone may not effectively raise educator salaries without additional safeguards for skills development. Consequently, the sector will continue under existing support measures rather than a standalone voucher system. The Government confirms its commitment to expanding affordable, quality childcare while addressing wage concerns through broader sector-wide strategies rather than shifting to a child-funded model.

results.title: Impact of F1 Singapore Grand Prix on Businesses
results.summary: Government agencies, including the Singapore Tourism Board (STB) and Land Transport Authority, regularly engage business stakeholders, particularly in Marina Bay, to mitigate disruptions from the F1 Singapore Grand Prix. Mitigation measures include improved wayfinding, ensuring mall accessibility via public transport and taxis, and reducing road closure periods from 12 days in 2008 to seven days currently. The STB's Grand Prix Season Singapore (GPSS) initiative offers 10 days of stakeholder-led festivities to help businesses capitalise on increased footfall and incremental tourism receipts. Since the race's inception in 2008, it has attracted over 720,000 international visitors and generated more than $2.2 billion in tourism receipts across hospitality, food and beverage, attractions, and retail sectors. Agencies and the race promoter will continue exploring ways to support businesses in deriving greater benefits while minimising operational disruptions.

results.title: Impact of Recent Education Reforms on Teachers' Workload
results.summary: The Ministry of Education (MOE) monitors teachers' workload via regular surveys, noting that average weekly work hours have remained stable over the past five years. To support adaptation to reforms like subject-based banding and the "Transforming Education through Technology" Masterplan 2030, MOE provides implementation guidelines, training workshops, and centrally deployed artificial intelligence tools for lesson planning and marking. Schools receive consultancy support and flexibility on implementation timelines, allowing a four-year onboarding period for the Masterplan 2030 to accommodate teacher readiness. Lessons from pilot schools inform resource allocation for Full Subject-Based Banding, while collaboration platforms facilitate the sharing of good practices to reduce change management burdens.

results.title: Impact of Singapore's Dependency on Natural Gas Market on Electricity Tariffs for Consumers
results.summary: Singapore's regulated electricity tariff rose 0.3% in Q4 2025, reflecting its status as a price-taker where over 95% of energy needs rely on imported natural gas. Since Q3 2022, tariffs have generally declined by approximately 9% as fuel costs eased. The Ministry notes that increasing renewable energy share could reduce exposure to fuel cost volatility, having already deployed 1.64 GWp of domestic solar by Q1 2025, surpassing the 1.5 GWp 2025 target. However, Singapore's small size limits domestic renewable deployment, meaning natural gas will remain a major fuel source while exploring other low-carbon options. The long-term impact of transitioning to low-carbon energy on electricity prices remains uncertain, depending on the specific sources introduced and their timing.

results.title: Impact of Smart Facilities Management System on School Staff in Facilities Management and Potential Re-employment Support
results.summary: The Minister for Education clarified that the Smart Facilities Management (SFM) system is designed to support, not replace, facilities management officers (FMOs) by enabling real-time monitoring of infrastructure and utility consumption. This transition aims to reduce manual tasks, benefit the ageing workforce, and improve building performance through predictive maintenance and energy optimisation. To facilitate the change, the Ministry will onboard FMOs onto the new system while providing training to upskill and reskill them. No redundancies were indicated, and the initiative focuses on enhancing the school environment for students and teachers.

results.title: Implementation of IMH's Suicide Warning Framework in School and Polyclinic SOPs and Data on Gatekeeper Training Programme
results.summary: The Coordinating Minister for Social Policies and Minister for Health stated that the question regarding the incorporation of IMH's acute suicide warning sign framework into school and polyclinic SOPs, along with data on gatekeeper training coverage and clinical contact timelines, was addressed in the oral reply to Parliamentary Question No 14 during the 14 October 2025 Sitting. No new measures, timelines, or specific statistics were provided in this session as the matter was already covered in the previous parliamentary record.

results.title: Improving Staff-to-children Ratio for MOE-run Kindergartens and Government-supported Preschools
results.summary: The Minister clarified that staff-to-children ratios for MOE-run kindergartens and Government-supported preschools represent minimum safety requirements, with facilities often operating with higher staffing levels. While improving educator well-being extends beyond ratio adjustments, the Early Childhood Development Agency (ECDA) has implemented several measures effective from 2024 and 2025. These include removing Saturday operating requirements, designating Teachers' Day and Children's Day as holidays, appointing relief staff providers since October 2024, and launching professional development programmes to support career progression. A detailed reply regarding these initiatives was previously provided on 2 July 2024.

results.title: Increasing Day Activity Centre Subsidies for Single-income Households
results.summary: Day Activity Centre (DAC) subsidies are means-tested based on per capita household income, prioritizing lower-income clients. While specific increases for single-income households were not detailed, the Minister noted that more families can expect increased subsidies for long-term care services starting July 2026, as announced in the Ministry of Social and Family Development's Committee of Supply earlier this year. Additionally, DAC operators may provide further financial support to assist families facing financial need where possible.

results.title: Installation of Anti-bird Netting on Town Council Building Facades
results.summary: Installation of anti-bird netting on Town Council building facades does not require Singapore Civil Defence Force approval or a Qualified Person submission. However, the Fire Code mandates that materials must possess limited combustibility and installation must not contravene existing requirements. Prevailing industry standards for these materials are explicitly stated within the Fire Code. The Ministry has not issued separate guidance beyond these statutory provisions.

results.title: Integrating Public Health Apps into One "Front Door" with Common Design and Interoperability
results.summary: On 7 March 2025, the Ministry of Health announced plans to consolidate HealthHub and three Cluster apps into a single application by 2027. The initiative aims to create an inclusive digital health front door with features such as support for Singapore's four official languages and compatibility with assistive technologies like VoiceOver. The Minister stated that functionalities and user interfaces will continue to be reviewed as part of ongoing improvements. Specific timelines for full interoperability or key performance indicators were not detailed in the response.

results.title: Investigations on Fatal Wall Collapse at Toh Close on 17 September 2025 and Review of Current Safety Requirements
results.summary: The Ministry of Manpower (MOM) and Building and Construction Authority (BCA) are investigating the fatal wall collapse at Toh Close on 17 September 2025, with no further details available while inquiries continue. BCA inspections found no structural safety concerns for surrounding buildings, though the Qualified Person must stabilise the retaining wall before work resumes. Existing WSH (Construction) Regulations already require contractors to check surrounding structures and provide adequate shoring. Upon conclusion, MOM and the Workplace Safety and Health Council will share key learning points and review whether additional safety measures beyond current regulations are necessary.

results.title: Key Factors Contributing to Lapses Reported in AGO Report in Recent Years
results.summary: The Minister for Finance stated that while the Auditor-General's Office (AGO) system remains robust, lapses and irregularities in recent years stem from human errors and process gaps inherent in a rapidly changing environment with high transaction volumes and complex IT systems. The Government treats all AGO observations seriously, committing to rectify identified gaps, strengthen governance, and adapt to operational changes through cross-agency coordination, change management, and staff adoption. To build capability and prevent recurrences, the administration emphasizes professional training in technical skills and judgement alongside the implementation of robust processes and controls. Public communication of rectifications occurs via AGO reports detailing issues and responses, supplemented by public statements when the nature and extent of an issue warrants it to uphold public trust.

results.title: Landlords' Asymmetrical Advantage over SMEs in Negotiating Rent Hikes
results.summary: The Deputy Prime Minister confirmed the Government considers landlords' asymmetrical advantage in rent negotiations, citing tenant sunk costs as a concern. Under the mandatory Code of Conduct for Leasing of Retail Premises, enforced by the Lease Agreements for Retail Premises Act 2023, landlords are prohibited from charging electricity mark-ups or using discretionary pricing formulas that increase rent. The Fair Tenancy Industry Committee develops and monitors this Code to ensure balanced lease negotiations. The Government will continue collaborating with the Committee to update the Code as needed to maintain fairness in the retail leasing sector.

results.title: Long-term Impact of MOE Kindergarten Expansion on Economic Viability and Diversity of Kindergarten Sector
results.summary: The Ministry of Education plans to open 60 MOE Kindergartens by 2029 to supplement existing preschools, aiming to uplift the sector through the Nurturing Early Learners Framework and support for lower-income families. While the Partner Operator scheme was initially intended for kindergartens in 2019, the Economic Development Council Agency will not extend it to this sector due to falling demand for half-day services and sufficient current capacity from Anchor Operator and MOE Kindergartens. Consequently, ECDA is prioritizing full-day childcare expansion to meet the needs of dual-income families, with parents preferring half-day options directed toward existing childcare operators. The Government maintains that current arrangements provide adequate affordable places while adapting policies to the evolving landscape of full-day care demand.

results.title: Making Online Reporting More Prominent on CNB Homepage
results.summary: The Coordinating Minister for National Security and Minister for Home Affairs confirmed that the Central Narcotics Bureau (CNB) will consider making online reporting more prominent on its homepage. Currently, the public can report suspected drug activity via the 999 emergency line, the 24-hour hotline at 1800-325-6666, or the non-urgent feedback portal at https://eservices.cnb.gov.sg/feedback. The Minister stated that CNB is reviewing the suggestion to enhance the visibility of the website reporting channel. No specific timeline for implementation was provided.

results.title: Mandatory Safety Protocols and Training Regarding High-risk Tasks Provided to Migrant Domestic Workers
results.summary: First-time Migrant Domestic Workers must attend the Settling-In Programme before work, covering safe execution of high-risk tasks like window cleaning and high-rise laundry in native languages. Materials were reviewed in May 2025, with further reviews pending impact assessment. New employers must complete the Employers' Orientation Programme and sign a safety agreement restricting exterior window pane cleaning. Employment agencies also provide pre-arrival safety training for common household tasks. The Ministry will monitor the latest changes before undertaking additional protocol reviews.

results.title: Measures to Support Families of Workplace Death Victims and Plans to Regulate Provision of Financial, Legal and Psychological Assistance
results.summary: Under the Work Injury Compensation Act (WICA), next of kin of deceased workers receive lump-sum compensation up to $225,000 without filing a civil suit, with the limit increasing to $269,000 from 1 November 2025. Employers failing to provide compensation face fines up to $15,000 or imprisonment of up to 12 months, while the Ministry of Manpower assists eligible claimants via the Workers' Fund if employers cannot pay. Beyond WICA, families accessing basic living support may approach Social Service Offices, seek legal advice through the Legal Aid Bureau or Community Law Centres, and obtain psychological assistance via Mindline 1771 or the Institute of Mental Health. The Ministry regularly reviews compensation limits to align with inflation, wage increases, and healthcare costs.

results.title: Measures to Track Social Isolation and Improvements in Quality of Life and Healthspan
results.summary: The Ministry of Health monitors Age Well SG progress through operational metrics like attendance and outreach numbers, alongside long-term indicators including frailty prevalence, chronic disease prevalence, and health-adjusted life expectancy. While specific measures to track social isolation and objective targets for reducing it were not detailed in the response, the Minister noted that academic research will eventually assess project impacts. Consequently, precise benchmarks for social isolation reduction and quality of life improvements remain to be established through future studies.

results.title: Meta's Compliance with Implementation Directive issued by Online Criminal Harms Act Competent Authority and Enhanced Measures that Followed
results.summary: Meta must strengthen Facebook's anti-impersonation scam measures by blocking photos/videos of Government office holders, removing impersonating accounts, and prioritizing Singapore user reports. The Online Criminal Harms Act (OCHA) Competent Authority will assess compliance after Meta submits documentation by 31 October 2025, following the 30 September 2025 directive deadline. Similar requirements for other platforms will be considered based on the scam situation.

results.title: Migrant Domestic Worker Safety Incidents, Enforcement Actions against Employers or Agencies and Statistics on Insurance Claims in Last Five Years
results.summary: Between 2020 and 2024, an average of 35 migrant domestic worker safety incidents occurred annually, encompassing falls from height, unsafe ladder use, and window cleaning breaches. All involved employers faced enforcement actions, while three employment agencies were penalized for failing to secure safety agreements. Mandatory orientation programmes require first-time employers to attend Employers' Orientation Programmes and first-time workers to complete Settling-In Programmes covering safe window cleaning, laundry hanging, and fall prevention strategies. Employment agencies must explain Ministry of Manpower safety requirements and ensure signed safety agreements before deployment. The Ministry of Manpower does not track or publish data on migrant domestic worker insurance claim statistics or average payouts.

results.title: Monitoring Frequency and Severity of Recurring Water Leakage in HDB Flats and Prevention Measures
results.summary: The Minister clarified that HDB assists Town Councils and flat owners with complex, recurring water seepage cases through joint inspections, technical advice, and the Goodwill Repair Assistance scheme, which covers 50% of repair costs. The remaining 50% is shared equally between upper- and lower-floor owners. HDB is currently evaluating technologies like microwave scanning to detect seepage sources through concrete, aiming to improve diagnosis and resolution for persistent leakage issues. Responsibility for interior maintenance lies with flat owners, while Town Councils manage external walls.

results.title: Monthly Household Income Levels of Families Enrolled in ECDA Childminding Pilot Scheme
results.summary: The Early Childhood Development Agency does not collect income information from parents using its childminding pilot scheme because the fees charged are not means-tested. Consequently, the Minister for Social and Family Development could not provide statistics on the number of enrolled families with a gross monthly household income below $12,000 or a per capita income below $3,000 for households of five or more members. The pilot scheme operates without income-based eligibility criteria, preventing the tracking of specific low-income household participation levels.

results.title: Multiple Sourcing for Drugs, Consumables and Laboratory Services for Competitive Pricing in Public Hospitals
results.summary: The Ministry of Health aggregates procurement via the Agency of Logistics and Procurement Services and maintains contracts with multiple suppliers to ensure supply resiliency. Productivity extraction factors are not practiced internationally, so efficiency gains are not systematically mandated for patients. Since 2023, MOH shifted to capitation funding for public healthcare clusters to incentivize value maximization through productivity gains and preventive health investments. The Ministry continues monitoring expenditure and bill sizes to ensure healthcare affordability for Singaporeans.

results.title: Number of Self-employed Person Income Relief Scheme Recipients who Remain Subjected to Payout Clawback
results.summary: Approximately 700 Self-Employed Person Income Relief Scheme (SIRS) recipients remain subject to payout clawback as processing continues. Delays stem from recipients failing to respond to Ministry of Manpower repayment requests, pending appeals for financial assistance, and approved instalment arrangements or extended deadlines. The Ministry assesses individual circumstances for those demonstrating repayment difficulties on a case-by-case basis. No one-time waiver or blanket financial support was proposed in the current response.

results.title: Old Voting District Reflected with Change of Physical Identity Card
results.summary: A change in a voter's physical NRIC after the 1 February 2025 cut-off date does not automatically alter their registered constituency. Instead, voter addresses are updated periodically through the Registers of Electors (REs) revision exercise based on government records as of the prescribed legislative date. This approach safeguards voter roll integrity and ensures certainty for candidates regarding the number of eligible voters per constituency. Consequently, an updated NRIC issued post-cut-off will not revert a voter's district to an old one unless an address revision occurs.

results.title: Oversight of Deviations Made by Rail Operators from Train Manufacturer's Recommended Maintenance Schedule
results.summary: The Acting Minister for Transport stated that regulations governing rail operators' deviations from manufacturer maintenance schedules were addressed in a written reply for Parliamentary Question No 58 on 24 September 2025. The Minister did not provide new details on specific regulatory constraints or confirm whether the Ministry will review SMRT's waiver approval system following the Transport Safety Investigation Bureau's 31 May 2025 report. Consequently, the transcript does not contain updated information on engineering justification requirements, documentation standards, or the status of the proposed review.

results.title: Passing of Night Blindness Test as Requirement for Obtaining Driving Licence
results.summary: The current driving licence visual requirements do not include a specific test for night blindness. Applicants must instead declare any disease or physical disability, such as night blindness, that significantly impairs driving ability; false declarations are offences. If a declaration is made, a licence is denied unless the applicant undergoes a medical test demonstrating fitness to drive. The Traffic Police assess the existing regime for new licence applications as adequate for road safety.

results.title: Pegging Remuneration of Rail Operators' Key Executives to Rail Performance
results.summary: The Acting Minister for Transport stated that rail operators are held accountable for service and reliability standards through penalties for disruptions and withholding of incentive payments for missing targets. As commercial entities, the responsibility for setting executive remuneration to incentivize outcomes like rail reliability lies with the operators' boards and management, rather than direct government mandates. The Government does not propose requiring remuneration pegging but maintains existing accountability mechanisms.

results.title: Permanent Residents with More than 180 Days of Stay Outside Singapore
results.summary: As of end September 2025, approximately 42,000 Permanent Residents (about 8% of the total PR population) had been outside Singapore for a continuous period exceeding 180 days. The Immigration and Checkpoints Authority does not track the number of PRs who have stayed in Singapore for fewer than 183 days or any specific duration within a given period. Consequently, no data exists regarding the count or percentage of PRs with a total stay of less than 183 days in 2024.

results.title: Postal and Delivery System Measures and Safeguards against Dangerous Parcel Threats
results.summary: Under the Postal Services Regulations 2008, prohibited items include arms, explosives, hazardous substances, and controlled drugs. Screening occurs selectively at borders, while SingPost staff are trained to identify suspicious signs like leaks. Upon discovery of suspicious mail, SingPost refers the item to the Police and Singapore Civil Defence Force for threat assessment and containment. These measures address the detection and interception of hazardous materials within the postal and delivery system.

results.title: Preventing Spill-over Effects of Unsustainable Private Insurance Practices into Public Hospital Charges and Keeping Premiums Affordable
results.summary: The Ministry of Health states that public sector care relies on clinical protocols and value-driven programmes, including Health Technology Assessments, to ensure cost sustainability and keep MediShield Life premiums affordable. While Integrated Shield Plans and Riders are designed for private healthcare and do not directly distort public costs, their overly generous coverage can indirectly affect the public system by driving patients seeking public care if private options become unaffordable. Managing private healthcare cost escalation requires collective efforts from doctors, hospitals, insurers, and policyholders to prevent spillover effects and stabilize premiums, as detailed in the oral reply on 24 September 2025.

results.title: Private Agencies Contracted for E-vape Enforcement Activities
results.summary: Enforcement against e-vaporiser offences is a whole-of-Government effort involving over 10,000 officers from various agencies, including the Singapore Police Force. The Health Sciences Authority (HSA) currently contracts approximately 100 staff from an external enforcement vendor to reinforce its team for these activities. The Minister clarified that there are no plans for the Police to replace HSA and its subcontractors, noting that the current multi-agency approach remains the operational strategy.

results.title: Progress Update on Enhancing Detection and Treatment of Occupational Diseases
results.summary: The Ministry of Manpower will expand proactive health surveillance from 1,200 workplaces in 2020 to 3,500 by 2025 to support occupational disease prevention.
